tluaSelfType.tlua(36,16): error TLUA100056: A 'self' type is available only in a member of an interface.


==== tluaSelfType.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // `self` is a receiver-relative type inside an interface. Inherited members
    // substitute the most-derived interface, which keeps fluent chains precise.
    
    interface HexZodSchema<T> {
    	parse(self: self, value: unknown): T;
    }
    
    interface HexZodStringSchema extends HexZodSchema<string> {
    	min(self: self, length: number, message?: string): self;
    	max(self: self, length: number, message?: string): self;
    	trim(length: number): self;
    	copy(): self;
    	peer: self;
    }
    
    interface BrandedStringSchema extends HexZodStringSchema {
    	brand(self: self): string;
    }
    
    declare schema: BrandedStringSchema;
    
    local minResult: BrandedStringSchema = schema:min(1);
    local maxResult: BrandedStringSchema = schema.max(schema, 10);
    local trimResult: BrandedStringSchema = schema.trim(1);
    local copyResult: BrandedStringSchema = schema.copy();
    local peerResult: BrandedStringSchema = schema.peer;
    local brand: string = schema:min(1):brand();
    
    // The contextual keyword remains a valid value binding and property name.
    local self = schema;
    local selfResult: BrandedStringSchema = self;
    local holder = { self = schema };
    local propertyResult: BrandedStringSchema = holder.self;
    
    // `self` is contextual type syntax rather than a globally available type.
    type Outside = self;
                   ~~~~
!!! error TLUA100056: A 'self' type is available only in a member of an interface.
